title: Solak: Why Crosby fits perfectly with the Ravens' D -- and their Super Bowl window
content: Baltimore had to give up two first-round picks to get him, but Crosby can dominate in the Ravens' defense.

title: Sources: Ravens to acquire Crosby in blockbuster
content: The Ravens have won the Maxx Crosby sweepstakes, beating out the Cowboys and others by sending 2026 and 2027 first-round picks to the Raiders for the star pass rusher, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Miami (Ohio) caps 31-0 season with overtime win
content: Miami (Ohio) finished the regular season as the only unbeaten team in Division I men's basketball, winning 110-108 in overtime over Ohio.

title: Skubal: No long-term Tigers offer, focused on '26
content: Tarik Skubal told USA Today Sports that he never received a long-term offer from the Tigers this offseason.

title: U.S. plates 15 in big win vs. Brazil to open WBC
content: Aaron Judge hit a two-run homer and Brice Turang had three hits and four RBIs to lead the United States to a 15-5 win over Brazil in its World Baseball Classic opener.

title: Exhausted Wemby emotional after 25-point rally
content: Victor Wembanyama said he had "zero left" physically but was all smiles after the Spurs overcame a 25-point deficit to stun the Clippers 116-112.

title: Trump plans 'all-encompassing' college sports EO
content: President Donald Trump says he will write an executive order within a week that will "solve all of the problems" brought forth in an unprecedented meeting at the White House to address the future of college sports.

title: Celts' Tatum calls 15-point return 'really good step'
content: Celtics star Jayson Tatum said he felt anxious in his first game in 298 days but was able to relax and finished with 15 points, 12 rebounds and seven assists in a 120-100 win over the Mavericks.

title: Source: Patriots trading center Bradbury to Bears
content: The Bears found a new starting center, acquiring Garrett Bradbury from the Patriots in exchange for a 2027 fifth-round pick, a source told ESPN.

content: As NFL free agency begins next week, we picked out five free agents who might be flying under the radar -- and five who are getting too much hype.

content: The star freshman has led teams to championships throughout his basketball career. Can he do it again for Duke?

content: The Tar Heels will miss their star freshman as the stakes rise, but his draft stock shouldn't plummet.

content: There aren't many tests at Duke that will produce a median score among takers of 43.2%. But Krzyzewskiville's annual "ordering test" isn't just any test.

content: Vanderbilt's pursuit of a 1-seed is over. But as many as four teams still have a shot at the final No. 1 seed.

content: Who are the best quarterbacks in the 2026 class? We sized up the top 12, starting with Fernando Mendoza and Ty Simpson.

content: Our experts make their picks for every round of the 2026 WBC -- from pool play through the championship game.

content: Grand prix starts have always held the potential for chaos, but this year there's almost guaranteed drama when the lights go out in Australia on Sunday. ESPN explains why.

content: George Russell delivered on his status as the world championship favorite by taking a crushing pole position.

content: Dallas Center-Grimes' Ava Smid caught fire at the game's conclusion to avoid elimination and punch their ticket to the state title game.

content: UFC 326 features a fight between Raul Rosas Jr. and Rob Font in an ultimate battle of youth vs. experience.

content: Liverpool's aggressive win over Wolves in the FA Cup shows that Liverpool can still turn back the clock, despite their recent struggles.

content: Tottenham Hotspur could replace manager Igor Tudor soon, while Barcelona eye Spurs' most promising young defender. Transfer Talk has the latest.

content: From 36-year-old legend Oksana Masters to 16-year-old Meg Gustafson, here's who to know as the Paralympics begin.

content: The initiatives focus on ensuring equitable access to basic needs such as employment and healthcare support, thereby creating sustainable livelihood opportunities for the community

content: 59 pastors from Bahrain, 48 others from Dubai begin journey home after Israel-Iran war disrupts flights in the conflict-hit region

content: The major components include the development of Vyttila Junction, beautification of the flyover, development of Vyttila North bus stand, street‑edge improvements, Phase II of the mobility hub, and a food street

title: Vellapallam harbour project work stalled as approval is awaited for revised estimate
content: The work had slowed considerably due to technical challenges posed by the soil conditions and officials have submitted revised estimate seeking an additional ₹100 crore

title: PM endorses Speaker Om Birla as Lok Sabha gears up to debate no-confidence motion against him
content: PM Narendra Modi says Mr. Birla manages ‘everything with dignity, never insulting anyone, patiently enduring harsh words even when some arrogant and disruptive individuals occasionally create disturbances’

content: The programme was organised by the Kerala State Higher Education Council at Rajagiri School of Social Sciences, Kakkanad

content: Chief Minister’s statement on ghee adulteration part of a larger conspiracy, alleges the YSRCP leader

content: A huge crowd, including children, youths, couples, and elederly, turned up to celebrate the festival with fervour, applying colours and exchanging greetings

title: PM Modi congratulates Nepal Government, people for successful polls
content: PM Modi also said that as a close friend and neighbour, India remains steadfast in its commitment to working closely with the people of Nepal and its new government

title: Row over UPSC rank 301 as two candidates named Akanksha Singh stake claim
content: The row intensified after an admit card circulating on social media appeared to show the same name and roll number, further complicating the matter

title: 'Betrayed the country': Rahul attacks PM Modi again over US trade deal
content: Rahul said the entry of large and mechanised American agricultural corporations into the Indian market would put small farmers under severe pressure and could lead to "devastation" in the agricultural sector. He further alleged that the country’s energy sector had been compromised and claimed that US President Donald Trump had obtained Indian data through the agreement.

title: 'Sab same line chipka rahe hain': SKY shrugs off Santner's 'silence the crowd' remark
content: Suryakumar Yadav playfully dismissed Mitchell Santner's pre-final vow to silence the Ahmedabad crowd, calling such remarks a tired cliché.  He emphasized the positive dressing room atmosphere as India, the defending champions, prepare to face New Zealand in Sunday's T20 World Cup final, aiming to lift the trophy on home soil.

title: Angry Pentagon tech head: We do not need tech tools from companies who …
content: Pentagon's chief technical officer Emil Michael criticized Anthropic for restricting its AI chatbot Claude's use in defense systems. Michael emphasized the military's need for partners supporting autonomous capabilities, especially for rapid responses to threats like hypersonic missiles. Major tech firms will continue supporting Anthropic but exclude defense projects, highlighting a broader debate on AI in warfare.

title: 'Today, Iran will be hit very hard!' Trump hints at targeting more leaders
content: US President Donald Trump issued a stark warning to Iran, threatening severe retaliation for its "bad behaviour" and considering new targets for "complete destruction." Trump claimed credit for Iran's apology to its neighbours following intense US-Israeli strikes. Meanwhile, Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian apologised for attacks, though missile strikes continued, indicating internal divisions.

title: LuLu Group airlifts 80,000kg of Indian produce to UAE to keep supermarket shelves stocked
content: Dedicated cargo flights are delivering fresh produce to the UAE as regional aviation disruptions impact traditional supply routes. Retailers are arranging special shipments from overseas to ensure supermarket shelves remain stocked with fruits, vegetables, and other perishables, maintaining steady supplies for residents.

title: Google founder Sergey Brin reportedly linked to Billionaire Bunker
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is reportedly linked to a $51 million waterfront mansion purchase in Miami's exclusive Allison Island. While his name isn't on the deed, transaction documents point to his involvement. This move places him alongside other tech titans like Larry Page and Mark Zuckerberg, who have also invested heavily in Miami's luxury real estate market, driving up prices.

title: 'If he gets going': Ex-player names NZ star who could spoil India's T20 WC dream
content: India faces New Zealand in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup final at Ahmedabad. Despite India's recent series win, former Australian players Brad Haddin and Nathan Lyon back New Zealand. They highlight opener Finn Allen's potential threat and captain Mitchell Santner's bowling prowess as key factors for the Black Caps, who they believe don't fear playing in India.

title: 'Never depended on permission': Govt official on India buying Russian oil
content: India has clarified its energy policy is driven by affordability, availability, and sustainability, not external permissions. A government official says recent US waiver on Russian oil purchases merely eases trade, not dictates policy. Officials confirm secure fuel supplies with ample buffer stocks, highlighting India's diversified sourcing from around 40 nations, ensuring citizen interests remain paramount.

title: T20 World Cup 2026 closing ceremony: Date, time, performers and full details
content: India and New Zealand are set to clash in the ICC Men's T20 World Cup 2026 final in Ahmedabad on Sunday. India, the defending champions, reached the final after a close semi-final win against England, while New Zealand dominated South Africa. This marks India's fourth final appearance and New Zealand's second, with both teams aiming for a significant title.

title: Unions representing 700,000 workers ask Amazon, Google, Microsoft to reject AI for war
content: Employees from major tech firms like Amazon, Google, and Microsoft are demanding their companies reject Pentagon contracts that could compromise AI safety guardrails.  The open letter, spearheaded by 'No Tech For Apartheid,' urges leaders to refuse demands that would allow mass surveillance and AI-powered weaponry without human oversight.

title: Yami Gautam reacts to trailer of 'Dhurandhar The Revenge' starring Ranveer Singh
content: Excitement is palpable with the reveal of the 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' trailer. Yami Gautam has hailed this espionage thriller as a must-see, ensuring an exhilarating cinematic journey. Esteemed colleagues like Alia Bhatt and Hrithik Roshan echo her sentiments, praising the trailer's gripping energy. Read the full article to know more in detail.

title: 'I'm very sad': Prez Murmu objects to Mamata govt allotting smaller venue for Santhal event
content: The President said she was very sad about the last minute change in the venue and also pointed out how chief minister Mamata Banerjee chose not to meet her, which is against the set norm. Murmu on Saturday said the contributions of the Santals to the country's freedom struggle have not received due recognition, and asserted that the names of several legends belonging to the community were "not deliberately included in history".

title: 'Goal is to silence Ahmedabad crowd', says NZ captain ahead of T20 WC final
content: New Zealand, the good boys of cricket, are ready to take the big boys, India, on in their own backyard at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad. The crowd, noise and burden of expectations will be a challenge, and Kiwis are ready to embrace it.

title: 'Clutch is Hardik Pandya': All-rounder credits calm mindset for Bethell runout - Watch
content: Pandya’s direct throw at the striker’s end in the final over of England’s chase ended Bethell’s innings of 102. After the dismissal, the 32-year-old reacted with an animated celebration and tried to lift the team’s energy. Pandya later said the reaction came because his son Agastya and girlfriend Mahieka Sharma were watching the match from the stands, and he wanted them to experience his emotions at that moment.

title: ‘You’re an IDF pilot’: Israel shares POV video of jet flying to bomb Tehran; watch
content: An IDF pilot's cockpit video shows fighter jets flying towards Tehran, with the pilot stating they are en route to strike historic targets. This footage emerges amid reports of explosions in Iran and escalating conflict, including an Israeli F-35 downing an Iranian aircraft. The broader war has seen significant casualties and continued missile and drone interceptions in the Gulf.

title: 'Give him a break': Ex-India batter urges India to drop Abhishek for final
content: Former India batter Mohammad Kaif proposes a significant change for the T20 World Cup 2026 final. He believes out-of-form opener Abhishek Sharma should be rested for the summit clash against New Zealand. Kaif suggests Rinku Singh as a replacement, highlighting his consistent performances across formats. He also contrasts Sharma's inexperience in ICC events with Sanju Samson's established status.

title: When cost stops being the barrier: The new chapter of Ozempic injection in India
content: The upcoming patent expiry for Ozempic in India is set to dramatically lower the cost of semaglutide, making this once-premium diabetes and weight-loss drug accessible to more patients. This shift could reshape the market, prompting earlier prescriptions and potentially altering societal views on weight management and body acceptance.

title: Sarpanch gunned down at Punjab gym, 3rd village head killed since Jan
content: A village sarpanch, Harwinder Singh, was fatally shot at his gym in Moga district by unidentified assailants. Police suspect personal rivalry, though the victim faced criminal cases. The incident has taken a political turn with relatives accusing an AAP MLA of threats, leading to a refusal for post-mortem until the MLA is named in the FIR.

title: UP headmistress gets foot massage from Class 5 students during school hours, suspended
content: A government primary school headmistress in Chitrakoot district has been suspended. A video showing a student giving her a foot massage during school hours went viral. The incident occurred at the Naya Bazar school. The headmistress denies the allegations, claiming the video is doctored. An inspection report led to her suspension.

title: Middle East tensions force England to move World Cup camp from Abu Dhabi
content: England's women cricketers have shifted their T20 World Cup prep camp to South Africa due to security concerns in Abu Dhabi. Around 30 players will compete in an intra-squad series in Pretoria, split into two teams named after former legends. This move aims to intensify competition for World Cup spots.

title: Vivek Oberoi returns to Dubai, thanks airline company and UAE leadership
content: Actor Vivek Oberoi shared his heartfelt emotions as he safely landed in Dubai, expressing deep gratitude for the protective measures surrounding his return amidst rising US-Iran tensions. He praised Emirates airline and the UAE leaders for their commitment to passenger safety, relishing the joyous moments with his family and the comforting presence of his kids after a nerve-wracking journey.

title: Russian missile strike kills 7 in Kharkiv; air raid alerts sound across Ukraine
content: A Russian missile strike on Kharkiv killed at least seven people, including two children, after a residential apartment block was destroyed. The attack was part of a wider wave of strikes across Ukraine, with other regions also reporting casualties and injuries. Poland scrambled military aircraft in response to the ongoing Russian assaults.

title: Can Surya do what Dhoni, Kohli couldn't? This T20 WC stat vs NZ may worry India
content: India faces New Zealand in the T20 World Cup final, aiming to defend their title and become three-time champions. Despite a previous loss to South Africa, India reached the final after a strong semi-final win. New Zealand, however, holds a perfect 3-0 record against India in T20 World Cups, seeking their maiden title.

title: ‘Bahut dar lag raha hai’: Lucknow family’s desperate plea in Qatar
content: A Lucknow family stranded in Qatar shared a harrowing video of missile sirens and their daughter's cries, revealing the terror of being in a conflict zone. The software engineer expressed constant fear and sleeplessness due to the barrage of missiles. Back home, her mother anxiously awaits news, praying for their safety amidst escalating tensions in Gulf nations.

title: Kerala shocker: Human arm, toe found under coach of Alappuzha–Dhanbad Express
content: Shocking discovery in Alappuzha as cleaning staff found human body parts, including a right arm and toe, beneath a train coach on Saturday. The Alappuzha–Dhanbad Express was stationed at the pit-line yard when the grim find was made. Railway police have initiated an unnatural death investigation, seeking information from other states to trace the origin of the remains.

title: US-Israel conflict with Iran: Is India exploring alternative energy options amid crisis?
content: Amidst Middle East geopolitical tensions, India is bolstering energy security by exploring alternative sources, with Australia and Canada offering additional gas supplies. The nation's diversified import routes, with only 40% of crude passing through the Strait of Hormuz, ensure stable energy availability despite disruptions.

title: 'Wanted to come into our port': EAM Jaishankar on India's rescue of Iranian ships
content: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar stated that India granted permission for an Iranian warship facing difficulties to dock in Kochi, guided by humanitarian principles. He noted that another Iranian vessel, IRIS Dena, was caught on the "wrong side of events" near Sri Lanka and was subsequently sunk by the US Navy.

title: Iran Prez apolgises for strikes on Gulf states; rejects Trump's 'unconditional surrender' call
content: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian has apologized for recent strikes on neighboring nations. He stated Iran will not attack any country unless attacked first. This comes as an interim leadership council now governs the Islamic Republic. Pezeshkian also rejected calls for Iran's unconditional surrender. The foreign minister suggested the Revolutionary Guard Corps is operating independently.

title: ‘He came to save us’: Family says Delhi youth was lured on Holi, beaten to death by mob
content: A neighbourhood spat over Holi colours turned deadly in Delhi, resulting in the alleged beating death of 26-year-old Tarun Butolia. Tarun was reportedly lured back home by a call claiming his parents were under attack, only to be ambushed by a mob. The violence has a communal undertone, with neighbours from different communities involved.

title: Bhopal horror: Class 3 student lured, raped by neighbour; locals thrash accused
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Bhopal Rural where a 41-year-old man allegedly raped an 8-year-old girl. The accused lured the child into his shanty, trapping her inside. Her screams alerted a neighbor, leading to her rescue by locals who apprehended and thrashed the suspect before police arrived. The accused faces charges under BNS and POCSO Act.

title: Explained: How Iran war is driving sharp rise in oil and gas prices
content: Global oil prices have surged dramatically this week. The ongoing conflict involving Iran has intensified, raising fears of prolonged energy supply disruptions from the Middle East. Key energy routes and facilities are disrupted. This has pushed oil and gas prices higher across global markets. Consumers and businesses are facing rising fuel costs.

title: Attack Chakravarthy and his confidence drops, Ashwin warns ahead of final
content: Ravichandran Ashwin observed Varun Chakravarthy's bowling struggles, noting a dip in confidence when attacked early.  Despite a wicket, Chakravarthy conceded 64 runs in the T20 World Cup semi-final, a costly spell. Ashwin advised tactical adjustments and warned against bowling round the wicket to left-handers, emphasizing the need for improved length and line variations.

title: Why Bombay high court said Cross or statue of Jesus cannot prove conversion to Christianity
content: The Bombay High Court ruled that household religious symbols like crosses do not prove conversion from Hinduism to Christianity. The court set aside a committee's decision invalidating a student's Scheduled Caste claim, emphasizing that formal religious rites, not mere symbols, are required to prove conversion and abandon caste identity.

title: In rage after neighbour hurt her son, Trichy woman hurls puppies against wall; 2 die
content: A Trichy woman, enraged after her neighbor allegedly injured her son, killed two newborn stray puppies she mistakenly believed were being cared for by the neighbor. CCTV footage of the brutal act sparked public outcry. Both women have been arrested, with charges including cruelty to animals and causing injury.

title: Pakistan's Asim Munir meets Saudi defence minister: Is 'Islamic Nato' in play?
content: Pakistan's army chief Asim Munir met Saudi Defence Minister Khalid bin Salman amidst escalating Middle East tensions following Iranian attacks. The meeting discussed halting these strikes under their Joint Strategic Defense Agreement, highlighting concerns for regional security. This follows reports of Turkey seeking a defense alliance with Pakistan and Saudi Arabia, potentially forming an 'Islamic Nato'.

title: Cancer survivor’s perseverance pays off as he cracks UPSC
content: Sanjay Dharaiya says The Hindu newspaper was a constant companion and helped him immensely with his preparations.

title: PU college lecturers, principals oppose proposal to transfer functions of DDPUs to ZP CEOs
content: The proposal to transfer is not just a general administrative change, it is a serious structural intervention affecting the very fundamental principles of the education administration system, says the lecturers’ association

title: Andhra Pradesh is on the cusp of tech revolution, says Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu
content: Highlighting the transformative impact advanced technologies, particularly quantum computing and Artificial Intelligence, will have on governance, economies and everyday life, the Chief Minister says the State will soon have a quantum computer in Amaravati and will soon make a mark in its manufacture and supply to the world

title: SBI launches $500 million social loan to advance women's economic empowerment
content: This syndicated transaction is a milestone for SBI and the global ESG financing landscape, for the noble cause of women’s empowerment, the bank said in a statement

title: Siddaramaiah rejects charges of excessive borrowing
content: Chief Minister says debt burden within the limits prescribed by the Karnataka Fiscal Responsibility Act

title: President Murmu criticises Bengal Government for protocol failures at Darjeeling event
content: The State Government had also changed the venue of the International Santhal Conference, where the President was participating, citing fears of congestion

title: Elephant herd triggers panic in Chittoor village again
content: 13-member herd strays into habitations near Muttavallur in Gudipala mandal; forest officials launch kumki operation and prepare plan to guide elephants back to migratory route towards Karnataka forests

title: Ensuring child safety is a collective responsibility, says Union Minister Pemmasani
content: Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, Rajasthan, Madhya Pradesh and Andhra Pradesh together account for around 55 per cent of child labour cases, says the Union Minister

title: Uttar Pradesh SIR: Over 70 lakh forms for including names, 2.68 lakh for deletion in claims, objections period
content: To facilitate the process, the ECI arranged hearings at 5,621 locations across the State and set up help desks to assist voters

title: Most-wanted gangster Amritpal Singh detained in Moldova, claims Punjab Police
content: Punjab Director General of Police (DGP) Gaurav Yadav said Amritpal, who is linked to the ‘Jaggu Bhagwanpuria gang’, is wanted in multiple criminal cases in the State

title: Akhil Gogoi hints Raijor Dal may go solo in Assam polls
content: In cryptic social media post, Sivasagar MLA Akhil Gogoi suggests failure of seat-sharing talks with Congress.

title: Army holds ‘synergy’ conference with ITBP, BSF at Northern Command HQ in J&K
content: The conference, under the leadership of General Officer Commanding-in-Chief, Northern Command Lt. Gen. Pratik Sharma, focused on sharing insights into aligning strategies and capabilities to surmount emerging security challenges

title: Visually impaired JNU pass out, labourer's son, JKAS officer, from J&K crack UPSC civil services exam
content: Resident of Manzpora in the Naidkhai area of the north Kashmir district, secured an all-India rank (AIR) of 957, bringing pride and celebrations to the frontier district

title: Row over pet dog triggers scuffle at apartment complex in Bengaluru
content: The row began after a resident took his pet dog to a designated walking and exercise area, meant primarily for senior citizens

title: Coimbatore gang rape case: Mahila Court sentences all three accused to life imprisonment
content: They have been convicted of gang-raping a college student, aged 20, in a deserted area near Coimbatore International Airport on the intervening night of November 2 and 3, 2025

title: 295 persons fined for smoking in public in Bengaluru, police book 40 cases
content: Police inspected commercial establishments and shops suspected of illegally displaying advertisements for cigarettes and other tobacco products

title: NCP leader Rohit Pawar meets Kejriwal, seeks help to raise Ajit Pawar's plane crash in Parliament
content: NCP leader Rohit Pawar emphasised that if a prominent leader cannot get justice in Maharashtra, it is essential for the MPs and others opposing those in power to raise this matter in the Parliament

title: 130 Maoists surrender before Telangana Chief Minister in Hyderabad
content: They gave up 266 weapons  including INSAS (Indian Small Arms System) rifles, AK-47 assault rifles, SLR (Self-Loading Rifles), bolt-action rifles, .303 rifles, US-made carbines, 9mm pistols and other weapons

title: ‘I’d have been an aerospace entrepreneur if not come to politics,’ says Rahul Gandhi
content: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi emphasised the importance of physical exercise for maintaining health and productivity

title: Kerala Assembly elections: Excluded voters can apply for enrolment till nomination deadline, says CEC Gyanesh Kumar
content: Gyanesh Kumar says such persons shall submit application in Form No 6 of ECI along with relevant documents and that any complaints regarding wrong inclusion of voters on list can challenged by submitting applications in Form 7

title: Revive Badanavalu Khadi Centre as model rural industry rooted in Gandhian principles: Report
content: Report by a group of Gandhians points out that quality of the product needs improvement while the product design too needs attention, but felt that the biggest gap lies in marketing and brand building

title: Woman complainant moves SC against pre-arrest bail to Rahul Mamkootathil
content: Special leave petition, filed by advocate Subhash Chandran K.R. for the rape survivor, takes exception to the “unwarranted adverse remarks” made in Kerala High Court order of February 12 imputing doubts on her character.

title: LPG price hikes: Opposition targets government, questions government’s claim of adequate stock
content: The prices of domestic LPG and commercial cylinders were hiked by a steep ₹60 and ₹114.5, following an increase follows a rise in global energy prices because of the West Asia conflict

title: West Asia conflict: Stranded Keralite students anxious about returning in time for semester exams
content: Parents back home, worried both about their children’s safety and academic future, scour portals for flight tickets, while making panic-stricken calls to colleges for reassurance

title: LPG price hike: Mithun asks why petrol, diesel costlier in Bengal compared to other states
content: The price of domestic cooking gas was hiked by a steep ₹60 per cylinder as oil companies factored in increased energy prices in view of the West Asia conflict

title: Police seize 16gm of MDMA; four youth arrested in Vijayawada
content: On a tip-off, the Task Force police conducted raid on a house near Moghalrajpuram and arrested four youth, who were working in hotels and restaurants

title: ‘Approached from a point of humanity,’ Jaishankar on why India let Iran warship dock in Kochi
content: Jaishankar, flagging social media debates over the incident, called for people to ‘understand the reality of the Indian Ocean’

title: Man who attempted to open fire on police during ‘Operation Vajra Prahar’ arrested, in Vijayawada
content: Accused tried to escape by firing at police, but trigger might have got struck, said EAGLE IGP Ake Ravi Krishna

title: Around 90% voters in Odisha mapped with 2002 electoral roll: CEO
content: The CEO on March 6 said this mapping process is expected to be completed within the next 15 days, and the target is fixed to achieve at least 95% mapping

title: Anbumani alleges ₹500-crore irregularities in regulated markets in T.N.; seeks probe
content: He alleged that the officials allegedly split works into smaller projects below ₹5 lakh to bypass approvals and audits

title: FIR against Badshah in Haryana for ‘Tateeree’ song; raids being conducted to arrest him
content: Several police teams are continuously raiding various possible locations to arrest the accused, even as a formal notice has been issued to him to appear before the police immediately

title: Proposal for ropeway at Mullayyanagiri attracts criticism from environmentalists in Chikkamagaluru
content: They have opposed the proposal stating that any such development will harm the pristine nature and disturb the wildlife

title: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee calls for protests on LPG price hike
content: Speaking about the BJP led government at the Centre, she said that the Union government is relying on the support of N Chandrababu Naidu and threatened to overthrow the government at the Centre if  genuine electors are removed from voter list

title: Helicopter with U.P. Dy CM onboard makes emergency landing in Lucknow
content: The chopper, with five persons including Mr. Maurya, his aide Vivek and two pilots, had taken off from Lucknow La Martiniere College ground helipad when it developed a snag

title: AIADMK to protest against ruling DMK on March 13 over failure to fulfil transport workers’ demands
content: The protest will be spearheaded by former Minister D. Jayakumar and will be held in front of Pallavan Illam, the headquarters of the MTC in Chennai

title: Karnataka High Court allows engineering college to terminate service of faculty due to closure of course, directs payment of ₹40 lakh to each professor
content: Malnad College of Engineering, Hassan had appealed against a single judge’s direction to continuing their services till they attain superannuation as per AICTE regulations despite winding up the Automobile Engineering Department

title: Kerala man booked for calling PM Modi ‘traitor’ on social media
content: According to the police, the comment was posted under a social media post reporting that Prime Minister had crossed 10 crore followers on Instagram

title: MP Su. Venkatesan seeks relief for MSMEs hit by U.S. tariffs, urges RBI to ease NPA norms
content: Mr. Venkatesan pointed out that the impact is severe in Tamil Nadu, a major exporter of textiles, where the majority of units belong to MSMEs

title: Arrested when minor, High Court orders release, repatriation of Bangladeshi prisoner in jail for 21 years
content: The court directed the State to make efforts for the repatriation of the appellant back to his country of origin, Bangladesh

title: Telangana | Farming, rising temperatures drive electricity peak load to record levels; 11,129 MW power demand on March 6
content: Telangana districts with high agricultural usage recorded considerable increase in demand for electricity

title: India says to push ahead with Russian oil imports
content: The U.S.-Israel campaign against Iran and Tehran’s retaliatory attacks across the Gulf region have upended the world’s energy and transport sectors, causing a surge in global oil prices

title: Karnataka High Court upholds CBSE’s decision to debar student for two years from taking class 12 exams for carrying mobile phone inside exam hall
content: The student claimed that he entered the exam hall with a mobile phone inadvertently as he came late to the exam centre

title: A Brewer goes 30/30? A Giant wins the batting title? Bold predictions for every NL team
content: a year ago we predicted Milwaukee Brewers OFs Jackson Chourio and Christian Yelich would each go 30/30 . they did not earn an All-Star bid or get traded . we also predicted Philadelphia Phillies DH Kyle Schwarber would knock in 125 runs .

title: US Deploying 3rd Aircraft Carrier In Middle East: All About USS George HW Bush
content: The United States is reportedly deploying a third aircraft carrier to the Middle East. The USS George HW Bush completed its pre-deployment training on March 5, according to the US Navy.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Can A Gen Z-Influencer-Rapper PM Really 'Change' Nepal?
content: Observers say it's high time the former mayor evolved and grew, as well as became more mature, diplomatic and friendly with journalists, opponents and, most importantly, friendly neighbours like India and China.

title: "Loser Of The Middle East": Trump Insults Iran, Warns Of Big Strike
content: US President Donald Trump on Saturday labelled Tehran as the "loser of the Middle East" after Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian apologised to the neighbouring countries for attacks launched during its war with Israel and the United States.

title: Restrictions Lifted in Kashmir A Week After Protests Over Khamenei's Death
content: Life across the Kashmir Valley returned to normalcy on Saturday as the administration lifted week-long security restrictions and communication curbs.

title: Class 10 Boy Stabbed To Death By School Students In Madhya Pradesh
content: What should have been the end of a crucial school exam turned into a scene of horror in Madhya Pradesh's Damoh city on Friday when a 15-year-old Class 10 student was stabbed to death outside his school, allegedly by a group of fellow students.

title: Nepal Election 2026 Results Live Updates: Rapper-Politician Balen Shah's RSP Heads Towards Landslide Victory
content: Nepal General Election 2026 Results Live: Rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah's newly formed Rastriya Swatantra Party (RSP) was on Friday heading towards a sweeping victory in Nepal's first general elections since the violent Gen Z protests

title: Aravalli Panel "Completely Suppressed" Forest Survey Opinion, Supreme Court Told
content: The Supreme Court has been informed that the panel led by the Environment Secretary "completely suppressed" the opinion of the Forest Survey of India (FSI) while recommending the controversial 100-metre height definition for the Aravalli hills.

title: Rapper-Politician Balendra Shah Unseats Nepal Ex-PM In Own Constituency
content: Rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah unseated former Nepal prime minister KP Sharma Oli in his own constituency, Election Commission data showed Saturday, with his party also seemingly headed for a landslide victory nationally.

title: AI That Edits Your Photos Now Drops Bombs From Sky, And Iran Paid The Price
content: The entry of AI in warfare has changed how we assess battlefields. What took hours to take a shot at has become a second's job. It's automated.

title: Israeli Army Says Found No Remains Of Missing Airman In Lebanon Operation
content: Israel's military said Saturday it had carried out an operation in Lebanon to find remains of airman Ron Arad, but failed to uncover any trace of the navigator missing since 1986.

title: Opinion: China's Quiet Military Aid To Iran - And Why Beijing Won't Admit It
content: China officially refrains from highlighting any military partnership with Iran, referring to itself as a "responsible major power". Even so, joint drills, its alleged support to Houthis, and a growing defence footprint tell a more complex story.

title: "Never Depended On Permission": India On Trump's 30-Day Russian Oil Waiver
content: The Centre on Saturday said that India continues to buy crude oil from any source offering the most competitive prices, as tensions prevail along the Strait of Hormuz route because of the Iran-US-Israel war.

title: US-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: "Take Your Wish For Iran's Surrender To Your Graves," Tehran Tells US, Israel
content: US, Israel-Iran War News Live Updates: Iran's president said Saturday that a demand by the United States for an unconditional surrender is a "dream that they should to take to their grave."

title: 2 Girls Found Dead In Gujarat Temple Bathroom, Had Searched Ways To Die By Suicide
content: The bodies of two college students were found inside the bathroom of the Swaminarayan Temple complex in Surat, with preliminary investigation suggesting death by suicide by injecting poisonous drugs.

title: Watch: Israel Shares 'POV' Clip Of Air Force Pilot Flying To Iran Amid War
content: The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) has shared a short video on social media showing cockpit footage of a pilot flying towards Tehran. The footage comes as the war with Iran enters its second week.

title: 'On Our Own': Americans Stranded Amid Iran War Say US Offered 'No Help'
content: As of Friday, about 27,000 Americans have returned to the US since the war began, the State Department said. The vast majority of them made their own way out without US government assistance.

title: After Iranian Ship Sunk By US, S Jaishankar Explains "Reality Of Indian Ocean"
content: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar on Saturday spoke about India's decision to allow an Iranian naval vessel to dock in Kochi amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, describing the ships as having been "caught on the wrong side of events".

title: 52,360 people flown from Gulf to India till Friday midnight on 280 flights:' DGCA
content:
.

NEW DELHI: Over 52,000 people had been flown to India from the Gulf on Indian and Middle East airlines and charter flights till 11.59 pm (IST) Friday, according to the DGCA. Indian carriers had operated 184 of these flights, followed by 85 by UAE carriers and 11 charters. Indian carriers are operating as many flights as possible depending on the slots they get in the UAE where scheduled flights are suspended as of now. The situation remains very fluid in the region. Dubai airport was closed on Saturday morning. “All flights to and from Dubai have been suspended until further notice. Please do not go to the airport. Emirates will share updates when available,” Emirates said on X Saturday morning. Passengers flown from Gulf to India upto March 6, 2026, 11.59 pm (IST)
